text:
Nvidia just bet $6.5 BILLION on light-based data transfer to fix AI's biggest bottleneck ⚡

Photonics could replace copper wiring in data centers — faster, cooler, more efficient.

The AI infrastructure race is getting interesting. #AI #Nvidia #Tech #Innovation #DataCenters https://t.co/CAyldoD2Kp

Interesting upstream read through. CPO doesn’t integrate lasers into the package. Both Broadcom and NVIDIA use external pluggable laser modules (ELSFP standard) that feed light into the silicon photonics engines. Bailly uses 16 per switch; Quantum-X manages all 144 × 800G channels with just 18. ￼

10,000 Foxconn switch trays = ~160,000 ELSFP modules this year at Bailly-equivalent counts.

The ELSFP supply chain is a two-horse race. $LITE announced its largest PO ever for ELSFPs and $COHR announced one too. ￼ Both targeting volume into scale-up hardware by 2027.

AI Photonics

- $MRVL "CPO units [in the Market] from 1MM in 2025 to more than 100 MM by 2030"

- Foxconn: "We will ship around 10k CPO units this year and the shipment next year will grow several times" 

$LITE $AAOI $GLW

text:
Nvidia has committed at least $6.5 billion since March 2026 to photonics companies as it pushes to replace copper-based data links with light-based infrastructure for AI systems that are straining existing capacity. The investment spree spans $2 billion stakes in Coherent and Lumentum tied to multiyear supply and capacity agreements, a $2 billion investment in Marvell Technology focused on custom chips and silicon photonics, participation in Ayar Labs’ $500 million Series E, and a $500 million investment in Corning to expand U.S. fiber manufacturing capacity. At GTC, CEO Jensen Huang said co-packaged optics are central to Nvidia’s Spectrum-X Ethernet platform, as the company bets that silicon photonics will be key to scaling AI compute while managing power constraints.

FOUNDER: Jensen Huang

AMOUNT: $6,500,000,000

#VentureCapital #NVidia #JensenHuang #TradedVC

$LASR $EOS.AX $AVAV

Und jetzt kommts....Der INLEAP FASTLIGHT SHIELD Laser steht auf einem UGV aus Tirol 🇦🇹.
#WarOfDrones

Squeezed states of light represent one of the most fundamental concepts  in quantum optics. They play a crucial role in various fields of  science, quantum technology, and photonics, with applications in quantum  information processing, quantum computation, quantum sensing, and  gravitational wave detection. In this paper, we propose a plasmonic  waveguide for the generation of squeezed states via four-wave mixing.

text:
1.6T optical is the next interconnect generation, and  it’s blowing up because the rack-scale GPU/XPU clusters being built in 2026–27 finally need that bandwidth — and the volume inflection is hitting right now. 

1.6T needs 200G/lane SerDes, 1.6T DSPs, and the laser/optics (EML, CW for silicon photonics) to all be production-ready together. That convergence is happening in the 2026 window, which is why it’s inflecting now rather than gradually.

I guess demand is big enough that $CRDO will most certainly win business on this side  but it’s a new comer to optical DSP although not new to 1.6t outside optical

text:
$LITE - LUMENTUM GAVE BACK 20% FROM ITS PEAK ON ZERO COMPANY NEWS — THE OPTICS TRADE IS JUST DIGESTING

LITE fell ~10% today and is now down more than 20% from its recent high, but there's no company-specific catalyst — it's a sector-wide pullback across photonics names after a parabolic AI-optical run. Analysts still carry a Buy consensus, and demand for optical interconnects in AI data centers keeps building.

The structural case is the co-design problem: at hyperscale, moving a bit over copper now costs more energy than the compute it carries, so the interconnect flips to light. That's why co-packaged optics and external laser sources are the next bottleneck — and Lumentum sits on the laser and transceiver supply side of exactly that shift. The pullback is positioning unwinding, not a thesis break.

text:
$LWLG signed a real deal with a real foundry, and the stock is up around 250% this year on it. Here's the part the hype threads skip: it still has essentially no revenue.

A development agreement is a tryout, not a contract. The two get conflated on purpose.

The bull pitch is the "Qualcomm model": Lightwave's polymer modulator tech gets baked into Tower Semiconductor's photonics platform, every future customer becomes a potential royalty stream, fat margins someday. It's a genuinely elegant story. The problem is that every load-bearing word is in the future tense.

Targeting royalties. Years from tapeout. Someday at scale.

What exists today is a development deal, a handful of customers in late-stage prototyping, a list of earlier-stage maybes, and a roughly $730 million market cap pricing in a royalty river that hasn't produced a meaningful dollar. Photonics is real and important to AI; that doesn't make this specific microcap de-risked.

A great narrative and a pre-revenue balance sheet can coexist for a long time.

The tell is the recent action: down about 20% in a month even while up 250% on the year, which is what a hope-driven stock does, lurching on sentiment because there are no earnings to anchor it. The deal de-risked the science. It did not turn the science into income.

The technology might win. The stock is still betting on a future that hasn't shipped.

text:
There's now an ETF for photonics and optics, ticker $LYTE. When a once-obscure corner of tech gets its own ticker, the theme has officially gone mainstream.

That's a signal, and it cuts both ways.

The fair version of the bull case is real: moving data inside AI data centers with light instead of electrons is becoming a genuine bottleneck, and optical components are quietly essential to scaling the whole buildout. Packaging that into one fund lets ordinary investors bet the picks-and-shovels layer under AI without picking one fragile small-cap.

Light is becoming the wiring of the AI era. Someone built a basket for it.

The caution is timing. ETFs tend to launch after a narrative is hot, near the part of the cycle where the easy money already moved. The fund exists because demand for the story exists, and demand for a story usually peaks before the story does.

The product is a thermometer for the hype, not just the trend.

So treat it as both. Photonics genuinely matters to AI infrastructure, and the existence of a dedicated ETF tells you a lot of people already figured that out. Real theme, possibly late entry.

The light's real. Whether you're early to it is the part the ticker can't tell you.

text:
Nokia is bolting Nvidia GPUs directly into its cell towers, and the reason is a number: 200 milliseconds.

A factory robot can't wait that long for a cloud server to think. So the thinking moves to the tower.

This is the edge-computing thesis finally getting concrete. Instead of routing every AI decision to a distant data center and back, $NOK turns base stations into tiny compute nodes, running the inference where the signal already is. T-Mobile was the first US carrier to pilot it. Nvidia's CEO described a future where every radio network becomes an AI network.

The latency you can't engineer away is the latency of distance.

Underneath the headline, Nokia quietly rebuilt itself. Its $2.3 billion purchase of Infinera turned it from a reseller of someone else's photonics into a vertically integrated optical company with its own chips.

Optical networks grew 20% year over year last quarter. Management raised infrastructure guidance into the teens.

The unglamorous telecom name nobody discussed for a decade is positioning as connective tissue for AI at the edge. Whether the AI-RAN vision pays off is unproven, and the capex is real. But the bet is specific.

The cloud got all the attention. The tower is where the robots actually live.

text:
❗ONLY 9 Hours Left❗

[Last Call for Substack/Full article]

Pricing update — effective June 3, 2026 (11:59PM Pacific Time)

PhotonCap started with a simple bet: that there's demand for silicon photonics research that goes deeper than press releases and earnings recaps.

That bet has paid off.

What PhotonCap does differently:

This publication doesn't rely on a single source. Academic conference papers, company PRs, earnings calls, and industry news are cross-referenced and analyzed together. That's how you catch what others miss: when a PR tells one story but the paper tells another.

Here's what that looks like in practice:

Primary source analysis — academic conference papers and journal publications dissected through an investor's lens. Not academic summaries — what each breakthrough means for the supply chain and who captures the value.

Investment maps — supply-chain-level deep dives mapping who actually makes what, from laser sources to optical packaging. The kind of analysis you won't find in sell-side reports.

Portfolio and earnings — portfolio holdings shared on a quarterly or semi-annual basis, alongside earnings analysis that decodes what the numbers actually mean at the product level.

PC101 lecture series — free foundational lectures on silicon photonics, from waveguide physics to PIC design, in English and Korean.

From device physics to public markets — PhotonCap connects the layers that most research treats separately.

Where we stand — and where we're going:

PhotonCap has grown to well over a thousand paid subscribers and ranks #22 on the Substack Top Bestseller list. I'm grateful that this publication is now trusted not only by individual investors, but by institutional investors, hedge funds, and sell-side analysts — as well as fellow research publications including Citrini Research, FundaAI, ChipStrat, Vik's Newsletter, and Jason's Chip, who subscribe as paid members.

Going forward, PhotonCap will expand beyond photonics and optics to cover a broader range of companies — all through the same engineering-first lens. The approach stays the same: primary sources, physics-grounded analysis, and supply chain mapping. The scope gets wider.

New pricing — effective June 3 (11:59 PM, Pacific Time):

Monthly: $50/month (currently $30)

Annual: $500/year (currently $300)

Already a paid subscriber? Your rate is locked in permanently. Nothing changes for you.

If you've been considering subscribing, now is the time. Lock in the current rate before June 3.

Content offerings, format, and frequency may evolve over time as PhotonCap grows.

— PhotonCap

text:
🚀 8 ETF Đang Xây Dựng Tương Lai Của AI

AI không chỉ là NVIDIA hay ChatGPT. Đằng sau là cả một hệ sinh thái gồm chip, bộ nhớ, điện năng, quang tử, robot và không gian.

💡 FOTO — Quang tử & truyền dữ liệu AI
“Hệ thần kinh” của AI với laser, silicon photonics và hạ tầng kết nối tốc độ cao.

🧠 DRAM — Bộ nhớ AI
Đặt cược vào HBM, NAND và các công ty lưu trữ dữ liệu cho kỷ nguyên AI.

⚛️ NLR — Năng lượng hạt nhân
Nguồn điện dài hạn cho các trung tâm dữ liệu và siêu máy tính AI.

🚀 NASA — Kinh tế không gian
Vệ tinh, tên lửa và hạ tầng cho nền kinh tế ngoài Trái Đất.

🤖 HUMN — Robot & AI hiện thân
Đầu tư vào robot hình người và tự động hóa thế hệ mới.

🔬 EUV — Thiết bị chế tạo chip
Những cỗ máy tạo ra các con chip AI tiên tiến nhất thế giới.

💻 SMH — Bán dẫn toàn cầu
Tiếp cận toàn bộ chuỗi giá trị ngành chip.

⚡ GRID — Hạ tầng điện
Các công ty nâng cấp lưới điện để đáp ứng nhu cầu năng lượng bùng nổ.

🎯 Nếu AI là một cơ thể:
🧠 DRAM = Trí nhớ
💻 SMH = Bộ não
🔬 EUV = Nhà máy sản xuất chip
💡 FOTO = Hệ thần kinh
⚡ GRID = Hệ tuần hoàn năng lượng
⚛️ NLR = Nguồn điện
🤖 HUMN = Cơ thể robot
🚀 NASA = Không gian mở rộng tương lai

Đây là 8 chủ đề đầu tư đang nằm ở trung tâm của siêu chu kỳ AI trong thập kỷ tới.

text:
💡 $FOTO CHÍNH THỨC NIÊM YẾT – ETF ĐẦU TIÊN TẬP TRUNG VÀO TOÀN BỘ CHUỖI GIÁ TRỊ QUANG TỬ

Nếu chip là bộ não của AI, thì quang tử chính là hệ thần kinh giúp dữ liệu di chuyển với tốc độ ánh sáng.

🚀 $FOTO (First Trust Indxx Global Photonics ETF) đầu tư vào các công ty hàng đầu trong lĩnh vực:

🔹 Truyền thông quang (Optical Networking)
🔹 Laser công suất cao
🔹 Silicon Photonics
🔹 Linh kiện & vật liệu quang học

🏆 Các khoản nắm giữ lớn nhất:
• Lumentum ($LITE) – Laser & module quang cho AI Datacenter
• IPG Photonics ($IPGP) – Laser công nghiệp hàng đầu thế giới
• Coherent ($COHR) – Chuỗi giá trị quang tử toàn diện
• Ciena ($CIEN) – Hạ tầng mạng quang tốc độ cao
• nLIGHT ($LASR) – Laser hiệu suất cao
• Fabrinet ($FN) – Nhà sản xuất module quang quy mô lớn
• AAOI ($AAOI) – Tích hợp dọc trong chuỗi cung ứng quang học

📊 Phân bổ ngành:
• 40.7% Thiết bị truyền thông quang & module
• 26.1% Laser
• 17.3% Linh kiện & vật liệu quang học
• 9.0% Gia công sản xuất quang học
• 6.9% Khác

✅ AI tạo ra lượng dữ liệu khổng lồ
✅ GPU ngày càng mạnh nhưng kết nối đang trở thành nút thắt cổ chai
✅ Silicon Photonics giúp tăng băng thông và giảm điện năng tiêu thụ
✅ Các ông lớn AI đang đẩy mạnh đầu tư vào hạ tầng quang học cho Datacenter thế hệ mới

🎯 Nếu NVIDIA là bộ não của AI, thì quang tử chính là hệ thần kinh kết nối toàn bộ cơ thể AI.

Sherry thấy đây là một trong những chủ đề hạ tầng AI đáng theo dõi nhất trong giai đoạn 2025–2030 khi nhu cầu truyền dữ liệu tốc độ cao tiếp tục bùng nổ.

text:
Hey chat, let me put some respect on $ALRIB 🧵

Riber SA. French semi equipment maker. ~€300M market cap. 109 employees. global leader in MBE — the process used to grow semiconductor layers inside quantum dot lasers, photonics chips, and AI data center components.

You've never heard of them. that's the point.
The numbers→ revenue €40.3M, net income €5.2M, margin 13% → order book up 24% to €26.9M end of 2025 → fresh Japan production order in Jan 2026 (not even in the book yet)

ROSIE is the real thesis - Their new platform for silicon-based integrated photonics, compatible with 300mm production lines. first two units ordered in 2025. ROSIE 2 (dual-chamber) in production in 2026.

This is the format hyperscalers need. 300mm compatibility = plug into existing fab infrastructure. that's not a research tool. that's an industrial product entering a massive market.

Why it's undervalued - Every quantum dot laser in every AI data center optical link needs MBE - Riber is the global leader in that equipment. 
One company : 109 people, €300M market cap. analyst target still €6. stock at €15.70. analysts haven't moved since the AI photonics cycle started.

Upcoming catalyst June 17 shareholder meeting. €0.10/share cash distribution (+25%) proposed, payable June 24. Profitable company returning cash while deploying next-gen tech. 

This is what undervalued looks like before the market figures it out.

text:
I've been looking into ZBLAN optical fiber as it's one of the clearer cases for manufacturing in space. 

ZBLAN is a heavy metal fluoride glass fiber. It offers the potential for lower optical loss than conventional silica fiber and transmits across a broader wavelength range into the mid-infrared. These traits could support longer transmission distances and new uses in sensing and communications.

On Earth, producing high-quality ZBLAN has been difficult. Gravity induces convection in the molten glass during the drawing process leads to microscopic crystals and defects that scatter light and limit performance.

In microgravity, these issues are reduced. The glass stays more homogeneous during drawing. Recent work by Flawless Photonics on the International Space Station produced over 11 kilometers of fiber in about a month.

The returned samples are under analysis, and microscope comparisons show much cleaner microstructures than Earth-produced fiber.

Challenges remain. ZBLAN is more brittle than silica and has a larger minimum bend radius, which affects handling. The technology is still in the demonstration phase but commercial production is being planned,and the plan is to manufacture it in space.

text:
🚨 Scientists shrank a powerful femtosecond laser onto a chip.

This is not a laser pointer upgrade. It is a photonics platform breakthrough.

Ultrafast lasers produce extremely short pulses of light, often used for precision measurement, spectroscopy, medical imaging, atomic clocks, and chemical analysis.

The problem:

Traditional ultrafast laser systems can be bulky and complex.

In a Nature paper, researchers demonstrated an integrated mode-locked laser using an erbium-implanted silicon nitride photonic chip and a Mamyshev oscillator design.

The key result:
The chip-scale laser produced nanojoule-level pulses, could be compressed to 147 femtoseconds, and directly drove a broad supercontinuum without extra amplification.

Why it matters:

Not a consumer product yet.
Not replacing every laser system tomorrow.

Still advanced photonics research.

4007}, {"from_index": 4787, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 4792}, {"from_index": 4831, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 4847}]}, "text": "Copper can't keep up with AI. That's not an opinion, it's physics. Every data center being built right now is replacing electrical connections with light. NVIDIA confirmed it with $4.5 billion in direct investment. I mapped 25 public companies across the photonics value chain: Every AI cluster being built today hits the same wall. A hundred thousand GPUs mean nothing if the data can't move between them fast enough. Copper maxed out years ago and photonics replaced it: lasers, optical fiber, and transceivers that push data at the speed of light. The AI transceiver market doubled in two years. NVIDIA committed $4.5 billion across three photonics companies this year alone. This is where the infrastructure money is going. Here's the full value chain: 🔬 MATERIALS & WAFERS This is the bottom of the chain. Every laser and transceiver starts as a wafer substrate: indium phosphide, gallium arsenide, germanium, specialty glass. Nobody above this layer can produce anything without these inputs, and right now the most critical one, indium phosphide, is the tightest material in the entire AI supply chain. The gap between demand and capacity is getting worse, not better. I think this is the most asymmetric layer on the map. Investors chase the transceiver companies and ignore who grows the substrates underneath them. But NVIDIA is writing checks worth billions in cash and warrants to lock up supply from this exact layer. First link in the chain, last to get attention, and the one that chokes everything above it if it breaks. Tickers: $GLW, $AXTI, $IQE, $AIXA, $AMS 💡 CORE PHOTONIC DEVICES This layer converts electricity into light and back. Without it, zero data moves through fiber. NVIDIA dropped $4 billion into two companies here this year just to secure production capacity, and both of them joined the S&P 500 within weeks of each other. That should tell you how fast this went from niche to essential. The supply gap is not closing. The companies shipping next gen lasers at volume can be counted on one hand, and switching suppliers takes years of requalification. Order books stretch past twelve months. Every next generation GPU cluster consumes more of these components than the last, and no one can substitute them on short notice. I watch this layer more closely than any other. Tickers: $IPGP, $COHR, $LITE, $LASR, $SIVE 🔌 COMPONENTS & MODULES The companies here take raw lasers and detectors, package them into finished transceivers and modules, and ship them straight to hyperscalers. If the layers below are the engine, this is the vehicle that actually reaches the customer. Hyperscaler purchase orders land here. The revenue acceleration shows up here first. What I like about this layer is that you can underwrite it today, not in two years. These are businesses with signed capacity commitments and product already moving. The consolidation angle matters too: larger photonics players have already started absorbing standalone module companies, and whoever remains independent gains pricing power as options thin out. Tickers: $AAOI, $MTSI, $VIAV, $LPTH ⚙️ SYSTEMS & EQUIPMENT No company above this layer can manufacture a single photonic component without the machines built here. One of these names holds 100% of the EUV lithography market with zero competitors. Others supply the bonding equipment for co packaged optics or the process control instruments used across the majority of advanced packaging lines. If photonics is the gold rush, this is the layer selling the picks. My honest take: this is where the smart, patient capital parks. Equipment companies have pricing power and multi year order books that generate cash through full capex cycles. They attract holders who don't panic on the first pullback. The stocks don't run 1,000% overnight, but they compound while everything above them swings, and that tradeoff is worth more than most people give it credit for. Tickers: $ASML, $BESI, $ASM, $LPKF, $MKSI 🔍 TEST, METROLOGY & YIELD The most ignored layer on this map, and arguably the one with the cleanest business model. Every wafer, laser, and transceiver has to be tested and verified before it ships. As speeds climb and photonic devices get more complex, the testing challenge compounds fast. The industry is now constrained not only by what it can build but by what it can prove actually works. Yield is money. Better defect detection means better margins for every company upstream, which is why foundries keep buying test equipment even when they slash budgets everywhere else. These are capital light businesses tied to every unit of production across the chain. Last check before product hits the customer, and one of the few layers where demand doesn't cycle down when the rest of semis softens. Tickers: $CAMT, $FORM, $AEHR, $ONTO, $VIAV 🧠FINAL THOUGHTS The NVIDIA capital concentration tells the whole story. One company wrote $4.5 billion in checks to three photonics suppliers in a single quarter. That is a company locking down the one input that could bottleneck its GPU deployments: the optical interconnect. Returns across this sector have been historic over the past twelve months. But separate the revenue growers from the narrative trades. Some of these companies are printing real quarterly numbers that would impress in any sector. Others are carrying multi billion dollar market caps on sub $100 million in annual revenue. Same sector, wildly different risk. Every generation of AI infrastructure from here forward needs more photonics. Not less. The copper to light transition inside data centers is early. Co packaged optics is barely in deployment, and 1.6T transceivers are ramping with 3.2T already on roadmaps. VERDICT: ACCELERATION SCORE — HIGH
Today's configuration — thermal combustion convulsing at $96 oil, embodied intelligence infrastructure being built at record speed by Nvidia/Marvell/Navitas, and capital visibly migrating between formations — represents one of the highest-velocity transition moments the matrix has recorded.
The farwaters are not waiting for the Strait of Hormuz to reopen. The new formational productive forces are being built because the old energy base is unstable. That is precisely the dynamic Pulatov's matrix predicts: the new formation does not replace the old at peak stability — it emerges through the crisis of the old.

text:
US AFTERMARKET + MACRO RISK SCAN — June 4, 2026
Matrix Analyst AI | Pulatov Matrix Framework
OVERVIEW: THE SCHIZOPHRENIC MARKET
The US market is caught in the most vivid illustration of the matrix's central thesis: two formational forces pulling simultaneously in opposite directions. On one side — Nvidia, valued at $5 trillion, trading around $221–232 range intraday on June 3  (Robinhood) , with the AI/compute mega-cycle accelerating. On the other — WTI futures at $96.02 and Brent at $97.81, rising as the US and Iran traded new strikes, with the Strait of Hormuz still partially blocked  (CNBC) . This is not a "mixed market." This is the rodovye skhvatki formatsii — the birth pangs of the transition, playing out in real time on the ticker.
OLD FORMATION COLUMN: THERMAL COMBUSTION UNDER SIEGE
The thermal energy base of late-stage Capitalism is experiencing its most acute stress test since 2008. Roughly 14 million barrels per day of oil supply remain disrupted due to the Strait of Hormuz blockade, with around 850 million barrels of cumulative supply lost over the first two months of the conflict  (ING THINK) . This is not a "geopolitical risk premium" — this is a structural rupture in the energy column of the old formational matrix.
Brent peaked at $138 per barrel in April — the highest since 2008 — before pulling back to the mid-$90s on ceasefire optimism, creating a violent $90–$100 range with a $138 tail-risk scenario if the strait fully closes  (https://t.co/8hzPgV6wLe) . Saudi Aramco's CEO has reportedly warned the market won't normalize until 2027 if the strait stays shut past mid-June. Capital is pricing a reopening that hasn't happened. That is the definition of fragile.
The Democracy-Capitalist subject is responding predictably: global oil output expected to fall 6.9 million barrels per day year-on-year in Q2 2026, the largest quarterly decline since COVID-19  (World Bank) . Professional union rights — the freedom level of the old formation — become irrelevant when thermal supply is collapsing. Inflation resets all social contracts.
NEW FORMATION COLUMN: AI/COMPUTE SIGNAL INTENSIFYING
While thermal combustion bleeds, the new productive force is printing record valuations. Nvidia at Computex announced Isaac GR00T Reference Humanoid Robot for academic research, DRIVE Hyperion as the global platform for a robotaxi-ready world, Alpamayo 2 Super Open Reasoning Model for robotaxis, and Cosmos 3 as the open frontier foundation model for physical AI  (CNN) — all in a single product cycle. This is not iteration. This is the embodied intelligence column of the new formational matrix going vertical.
Marvell Technology surged to $290.79, up 32%, after Nvidia CEO highlighted its AI infrastructure role  (Robinhood) . Marvell acquired Celestial AI — a provider of Photonic Fabric technology for next-generation scale-up interconnect — for $3.5 billion in February 2026, with the acquisition expected to accelerate connectivity strategy for next-generation AI and cloud data centers  (sec) . Photonics is the new energy efficiency layer: it sits in the "New energy / power management" column of the transition. This is not a trade — this is capital voting for the next formational architecture.
CONFLICTS OF TRANSITION
The matrix identifies three live collision zones right now:
First, thermal shock vs AI-boom dissociation. The same session saw oil near $96 and Nvidia near $232 all-time-high territory. The old formational capital (energy companies, industrial complex) is being taxed by the Hormuz crisis while new formational capital (AI, semiconductors, optics) breaks records. This is capital migrating between formations in real time.
Second, capital volatility as formational stress indicator. Brent ran from below $60 at the start of 2026 to a $138 April peak before the May unwind — a range that reflects the system repricing into an entirely different energy regime  (https://t.co/8hzPgV6wLe) .

The Board of Directors recommends that shareholders vote "FOR" the approval of both proposals to support the company's business plan and potential future financing.

text: Typical zionist manipulation today on photonics what a great day to buy more i added to my $aaoi position

2026 is shaping up to be the year of photonics and optical infrastructure. 🚀

🔹 $AAOI — Supplies optical transceivers powering AI data center connectivity. High-growth potential, but results can be volatile.

🔹 $AVGO — Dominates custom AI chips and networking hardware. One of the biggest beneficiaries of AI infrastructure spending.

🔹 $LITE — Leading provider of optical components for hyperscale data centers and telecom networks. Recovery story with AI tailwinds.

🔹 $CRDO — Builds high-speed connectivity solutions for AI clusters. Growing rapidly, though expectations are already sky-high.

🔹 $ALAB — Emerging player in optical networking silicon. Early innings with a massive addressable market ahead.

🔹 $GLW — The backbone behind fiber and specialty glass. Less flashy, but quietly benefits from the AI buildout.

🔹 $COHR — Photonics and laser technology leader. AI opportunity is significant, but recent integration challenges have masked the story.

🔹 $AEHR — Semiconductor testing specialist expanding beyond EVs into AI, GaN, and next-generation chip markets.

As AI scales, moving data efficiently becomes just as important as computing it. That’s where photonics comes in. 💡

text:
$ANET Fundamental Deep Dive 🟢 Arista Networks

OVERVIEW

Arista Networks operates as a leading provider of networking solutions across AI/HPC infrastructure and routing environments. FY2025 earnings showed significant momentum, with annual revenue of $9B, growing +29% YoY and guiding strongly. Hyperscaler demand and Ethernet fabric expansion delivered the bulk of growth, supported by 400G/800G switches scaling into higher-speed AI clusters. This is only the start of this vertical and will drive growth for years. Excited to see how their 1.6T+ programs and continued AI ramps perform in hyperscaler data centers in 2H 2026 and beyond.

Arista provides the full stack, providing high-performance Ethernet switches with EOS software. This allows ease of virtualization. This company is at the forefront of converting silicon into deployable, rack-ready systems at volume for hyperscalers. They're the pure-play leader enforcing the 1.6T+ photonics wave. It's a significant reason for my comfort in investing in the company as a leveraged AI networking play.

The previous earnings calls confirmed 1.6T+ data center switches are now ready to order, with XPO liquid-cooled pluggable optic modules delivering up to 12.8 Tbps per module. This advancement delivers massive scale and efficiency for hyperscaler AI clusters, addressing power and density limits in next-gen fabrics. The campus and routing segments provide more stable contributions, though with focus shifting toward high-margin AI data center opportunities.

Profitability is set to explode in the years moving forward as AI products mix deeper into the portfolio. Non-GAAP operating margin reached new highs around 47%+ with strong EPS growth. FCF remains exceptional with minimal capital intensity. Competition is present with $NVDA as the behemoth expands into the space with Spectrum-X; however, demand is rampant and Arista is the reliable, proven leader that networking engineers love according to feedback.

------------------------------------

GROWTH OUTLOOK

$ANET sits at a $220 Billion valuation. Its valuation reflects strong growth expectations with a ~60x Trailing P/E and ~24x P/S Ratio, indicative of the stock sitting at a premium. In my eyes, it's entirely deserved and can easily expand considering it's a extremely high margin business. Leveraging their +25-30% sequential YoY growth, it present a unique opportunity as a pure-play "picks-and-shovels" company.

Arista Networks has consistently outperformed expectations. Management projects robust revenue growth over the next 3–5 years, driven largely by AI data center + Ethernet acceleration. Anticipate a smooth rise in valuation. Their TAM is significant and growing exponentially. It's an extremely comfortable long moving forward. Product roadmaps support this outlook, as shown by their unveiling of 1.6T+ systems and XPO liquid-cooled modules set to hit the the income statement 2H 2026 onwards.

Over FY2026, the company is projected to report ~$11.5B Revenue and $3.60 adjusted EPS annually. This will be followed by even stronger growth in 2027, implying visibility and penetration improvement.

Risks include cyclical demand, supply chain execution, customer concentration, and broader macro slowdowns in tech spending.

------------------------------------

FAIR VALUATION ESTIMATE

Using an aggregated/blended valuation approach based on many different disciplines of FA (DCF, Relative Multiples, Analysts, etc.),

Current FVE = $240/share

This does not take into account its future prospects and potential for technical momentum to accelerate price far beyond this FVE.

------------------------------------

LONG-TERM ESTIMATES

Assuming $ANET consistently meets aggressive growth targets (revenue ramp achieved with margins maintained), this is their potential path forward valuation-wise when using P/E multiples and expected EPS values.

EY2026 = $300+
EY2027 = $450+
EY2028 = $600+
EY2029 = $800+

All in all, another high conviction investment that can multiply its valuation safely and securely over the next half a decade.

"$ANET Fundamental Deep Dive 🟢 Arista Networks

OVERVIEW

Arista Networks operates as a leading provider of networking solutions across AI/HPC infrastructure and routing environments. FY2025 earnings showed significant momentum, with annual revenue of $9B, growing +29% YoY and guiding strongly. Hyperscaler demand and Ethernet fabric expansion delivered the bulk of growth, supported by 400G/800G switches scaling into higher-speed AI clusters. This is only the start of this vertical and will drive growth for years. Excited to see how their 1.6T+ programs and continued AI ramps perform in hyperscaler data centers in 2H 2026 and beyond.

Arista provides the full stack, providing high-performance Ethernet switches with EOS software. This allows ease of virtualization. This company is at the forefront of converting silicon into deployable, rack-ready systems at volume for hyperscalers. They're the pure-play leader enforcing the 1.6T+ photonics wave. It's a significant reason for my comfort in investing in the company as a leveraged AI networking play.

The previous earnings calls confirmed 1.6T+ data center switches are now ready to order, with XPO liquid-cooled pluggable optic modules delivering up to 12.8 Tbps per module. This advancement delivers massive scale and efficiency for hyperscaler AI clusters, addressing power and density limits in next-gen fabrics. The campus and routing segments provide more stable contributions, though with focus shifting toward high-margin AI data center opportunities.

Profitability is set to explode in the years moving forward as AI products mix deeper into the portfolio. Non-GAAP operating margin reached new highs around 47%+ with strong EPS growth. FCF remains exceptional with minimal capital intensity. Competition is present with $NVDA as the behemoth expands into the space with Spectrum-X; however, demand is rampant and Arista is the reliable, proven leader that networking engineers love according to feedback.

------------------------------------

GROWTH OUTLOOK

$ANET sits at a $220 Billion valuation. Its valuation reflects strong growth expectations with a ~60x Trailing P/E and ~24x P/S Ratio, indicative of the stock sitting at a premium. In my eyes, it's entirely deserved and can easily expand considering it's a extremely high margin business. Leveraging their +25-30% sequential YoY growth, it present a unique opportunity as a pure-play "picks-and-shovels" company.

Arista Networks has consistently outperformed expectations. Management projects robust revenue growth over the next 3–5 years, driven largely by AI data center + Ethernet acceleration. text:
-> IP acquisition
-> Just waiting for CPO to take off

$SIVE is a laser chokepoint for photonics and are publicly validated by:

- $GFS (1 of 2 public laser suppliers with $LITE for CPO per presentation)

- $JBL (“Relatively dramatic moat” for pluggables built with Sivers)

- CHIPS ACT for the overall company

For highest visibility:

-> Ayar is the largest CPO player that primary sources $SIVE and are expected to ramp in 2027.

-> $POET is another near term CPO volume player that’s heavily visible with Sivers.

For OSINT mapping:

$MRVL Celestial (direct, not through Poet), Lightmatter, Lightelligence, were all high confidence customers of Sivers. 

$SIVE is also developing/qualifying with multiple more optical transceiver players following Jabil.

It feels like they’re going to end up everywhere. 

I’m not sure people realize how special that many qualifications is coming from a <$2B MC laser company is… right before 2027 volume ramp. 

Especially while all the other laser companies trade at $15B-$70B valuations. 

Just need pluggables to bridge revenue gap into H2 2027 (CPO Scale up)

Then making every laser they sell more valuable following the $LITE playbook, to capture more TAM of both markets.  

- In the overarching optical $141B TAM (10x) in the next 1 1/2 years. (Goldman Sachs)

- and CPO TAM going from 0 to $81B in the next 1 1/2 years. 

So, easily multiply revenue opportunity overnight doing IP acquisition downstream. 

It’s more of just a waiting game, I think $SIVE is very undervalued relative to forward revenue potential. 

If it were a private Silicon Valley startup it would probably be worth $4-6B today.

Just needs to get listed on NASDAQ for premiums to bridge that gap.

text:
$SIVE CEO: “In the last 4-5 months we have received a lot of photonics opportunities to the level where both businesses (wireless and photonics) are challenging each other in terms of pipeline they occupy.”

In the past it was more focused on wireless. Now it isn’t. 👀

He also states that pipeline opportunities are at different stages. That’s why they are key to future revenue potential, as they are closely tied to execution - not just open numbers without a real path to production. 😉

text:
What’s so impressive to me about @joinautopilot’s “Photonics is Next” basket is that @michaelsikand + @KawzInvests & the folks at Autopilot were months ahead of the ETF wrappers now chasing the theme. $LYTE Photonics ETF from @roundhill is not yet listed, and $FOTO Photonics ETF just listed on May 29th. 

Before either existed, @joinautopilot provided the best pure-play photonics ETF exposure you could actually get. $EUV is the closest listed alternative, but it’s not a true pure play. It’s thematically diluted with semicap and foundry names like $TSM, $ASML, etc. 

By the time $LYTE finally lists, a lot of the alpha will likely already be gone. The growth is happening before the ETF shows up, not after.
Check $LITE & $AAOI YTD gain for reference.

Autopilot has proved it’s a serious platform for retail investors, not only a Congress copy-trading app everyone knows it for. Haven’t ever used Autopilot before, but I’m now open to the possibility. 

Bravo, @joinautopilot @michaelsikand @KawzInvests 🫰🫰🫰

(Appreciation post, not a paid promo)

text:
$sive CEO 인터뷰

1. 1분기 실적

- 환율과 미국 정부 셧다운 여파(정부 자금 및 국방 지출 승인 지연)로 인해 자체 목표보다 다소 아쉬운 실적을 기록함.

2. 사업 현황

- 현재 성장을 이끄는 무선(Wireless) 부문은 위성 통신 시장 확대를 준비 중이며, 미래 동력인 포토닉스(Photonics) 부문은 2027년 AI 데이터 센터 시장 진입을 준비하고 있음. 

3. 미국 상장 추진

- 미국 증시 교차 상장을 준비 중
- 규제와 비용 부담이 늘지만, 대규모 전문 투자자 확보와 장기적 자금 조달에 유리하며 이를 위해 재무 통제와 현장 인력 투자를 늘렸음.

4. 최종 목표

- 다수의 신제품이 본격적으로 대량 양산되는 2027년을 제품 폭발 성장의 해로 만드는 것이 현재 최우선 과제임.

text:
Overconfidence on X on memory, CPUs, photonics is getting high to the point where it’s time to start going on bubble watch (no, it was not time before). The obnoxious 2019-2020 voices are back again. 

There are many reasons for capital to rotate out of the AI / bottleneck trades that have nothing to do with the supply constraints. 2028-2030 expectations will simply be too far in the future to underwrite when there’s essentially been sectors abandoned due to Hormuz / inflation + consumer uncertainty. 

The appetite for sky high multiples is not infinite. Some of this capital will rotate into software first, then PE, then consumer and financials. That is a LOT of capacity to absorb institutional capital, even if retail wants to keep playing in the AI / space / drones + robotics ball pit. 

I don’t know if it’s months or quarters, but something will probably happen out of Asia (China sabre rattling on rare earths or other essential supply chain materials they can exert control over, regional recessions due to oil shenanigans, etc.) blowing up the market and unwind a decent portion of the gains due to the leverage on the AI trade. Institutions will leave first, and they will leave quickly (have performance fees and bonuses to lock in, and plenty of places they’d rather invest in if there were any evidence the rest of the market wanted to participate again).

text:
🌐🔥 THE FUTURE ECONOMY - MAPPED 🔥🌐

Everyone's arguing about which stock wins.

8 sectors - the infrastructure the next decade actually runs on 👇

💡 PHOTONICS -> light kills copper
$CRDO · $SIVE · $AAOI

⚙️ AI INFRASTRUCTURE -> the compute buildout
$IREN · $NBIS · $DGXX

🤖 PHYSICAL AI -> the eyes 👁️ the brain 🧠 the compute 🦾
$OUST · $AMBA · $OSS

🛸 AUTONOMY -> air, sea & machines that run themselves
$ONDS · $MRLN · $KRKNF

⚡ ENERGY & STORAGE -> powering it all
$TE · $EOSE · $AMPX

🪨 CRITICAL MINERALS -> the chokepoints China controls
$MP · $UAMY · $ASPI

🚀 SPACE -> the orbital economy
$ASTS · $RKLB · $PL

⚛️ QUANTUM -> the wildcard frontier
$IONQ · $INFQ · $RGTI

I left names out on purpose. Tell me which one I'm wrong about 👇

The screens get the attention. The infrastructure gets the returns. 💰

text:
A few asked whether I'm worried about the AI/photonics trade after the recent volatility.

not really.. we are in a digestion/conso phase..

If anything, I think people are getting distracted by short-term price action while the long-term capex picture keeps getting bigger.

McKinsey's baseline scenario alone calls for $5.2T of AI-driven data center capex by 2030.

my view hasn't changed: own the upstream.

when trillions are flowing into AI infra I want exposure to the companies selling the picks and shovels.

From optics/CPO ($LITE $COHR $AAOI $MTSI $AEHR) to Memory ($MU $SNDK) to foundries and compute ($AMD $ARM $TSEM $INTC) etc..

The market keeps debating who wins AI.

I'm more interested in who gets paid regardless of who wins.

text:
Ok , let’s keep going further …

The New Rise of the Photonic Industry

How Sivers Can Carve Out Its Place in Photonics : 

In AI data centers, the bottleneck is no longer just GPU compute power — it’s how data moves between them.

A modern GPU has thousands of cores processing data in parallel. 
Inside a GPU or between nearby GPUs, data is increasingly traveling through light (photonics). 

But between racks (a few meters apart), copper is still dominant. 

Copper consumes enormous energy and generates massive heat. 

At the scale of clusters with tens of thousands of GPUs, this becomes a critical bottleneck.

This is where photonics — CPO (Co-Packaged Optics) for the intra GPU data transmission and LRO for the inter rack data transmission — becomes essential: replacing copper with light dramatically reduces energy per bit and frees up power for actual computation instead of cooling.

The three main laser players in this revolution:

•  Lumentum (LITE) and Coherent (COHR): Leaders in high-power lasers (EML). Their strength is raw power, ideal for longer distances and inter-rack connections.

•  Sivers: Its strength lies in low-power multi-λ CW laser arrays (multiple wavelengths on a single chip). These lasers are particularly suited for highly integrated, ultra-energy-efficient architectures needed for intra-GPU or near-GPU connections.

The TAM is becoming massive.

As Marvell highlighted in a recent official post, the co-packaged connectivity market (CPO + NPO) is expected to grow from under 1 million ports in 2025 to over 100 million ports per year by 2030. That’s explosive growth !! 

https://t.co/zB6TU3IjNy

There is very little chance that one or two players alone can handle such massive demand. The market will need multiple suppliers and multiple architectures (high-power for inter-rack + low-power for intra/near-GPU).

We can therefore imagine a natural market split:

•  Lumentum and Coherent will take a very large share of inter-rack connections (longer distances, massive volumes).
•  Sivers, with its advantage in low power consumption and multi-λ integration, can capture a significant portion of intra-GPU / near-GPU connections (the most critical segment for energy efficiency in future clusters).

Photonics is at the dawn of a new era that promises to be bright.

And Sivers, despite its small size, has a real card to play in this supercycle.

"indices": [1617, 1640], "url": "https://t.co/zB6TU3IjNy"}], "user_mentions": []}, "id": "Tm90ZVR3ZWV0OjIwNjExMDE2MzUyODQxNDAwMzI=", "text": "Ok , let’s keep going further … The New Rise of the Photonic Industry How Sivers Can Carve Out Its Place in Photonics : In AI data centers, the bottleneck is no longer just GPU compute power — it’s how data moves between them. A modern GPU has thousands of cores processing data in parallel. Inside a GPU or between nearby GPUs, data is increasingly traveling through light (photonics). But between racks (a few meters apart), copper is still dominant. Copper consumes enormous energy and generates massive heat. At the scale of clusters with tens of thousands of GPUs, this becomes a critical bottleneck. This is where photonics — CPO (Co-Packaged Optics) for the intra GPU data transmission and LRO for the inter rack data transmission — becomes essential: replacing copper with light dramatically reduces energy per bit and frees up power for actual computation instead of cooling. The three main laser players in this revolution: • Lumentum (LITE) and Coherent (COHR): Leaders in high-power lasers (EML). Their strength is raw power, ideal for longer distances and inter-rack connections. • Sivers: Its strength lies in low-power multi-λ CW laser arrays (multiple wavelengths on a single chip). These lasers are particularly suited for highly integrated, ultra-energy-efficient architectures needed for intra-GPU or near-GPU connections. The TAM is becoming massive. As Marvell highlighted in a recent official post, the co-packaged connectivity market (CPO + NPO) is expected to grow from under 1 million ports in 2025 to over 100 million ports per year by 2030. That’s explosive growth !! https://t.co/zB6TU3IjNy There is very little chance that one or two players alone can handle such massive demand. The market will need multiple suppliers and multiple architectures (high-power for inter-rack + low-power for intra/near-GPU). We can therefore imagine a natural market split: • Lumentum and Coherent will take a very large share of inter-rack connections (longer distances, massive volumes). • Sivers, with its advantage in low power consumption and multi-λ integration, can capture a significant portion of intra-GPU / near-GPU connections (the most critical segment for energy efficiency in future clusters). Photonics is at the dawn of a new era that promises to be bright. And Sivers, despite its small size, has a real card to play in this supercycle. #SIVE #Sivers #SIVEF #Photonics #CPO #CoPackagedOptics #AyarLabs #Jabil #Lumentum #Coherent #Marvell #AI #ArtificialIntelligence #DataCenter #Semiconductors #SiliconPhotonics #LowPower #Laser #Beamforming #SATCOM #Defense #TechStocks #GrowthStock #Investing #Stocks #Nvidia #OpenAI #Oracle #Wiwynn#Marvell"}}}, "previous_counts": {"bookmark_count": 0, "favorite_count": 0, "quote_count": 0, "reply_count": 0, "retweet_count": 0}, "rest_id": "2061101635577708984", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "110", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
The smartest capital doesn’t chase the trend. It maps the bottlenecks the trend creates and then buys the one the crowd hasn’t reached yet.

AI needs compute. Compute needs photonics and memory. Photonics needs substrates. All of it needs power. Power needs grid and nuclear. The data needs to move -> networking, connectivity. The physical world needs to act on it -> robotics, autonomy, defense. And the whole stack increasingly runs through space.

Each layer is a chokepoint. Each chokepoint is a trade.

The trend is obvious. The bottleneck three layers down isn’t. That’s where the asymmetry lives.

text:
I wrote up my full thoughts on $SIVE / $SIVEF

The TLDR:

> I think their gross margin target is great
> I think their AI Data Center Photonics pipeline opportunity is very low, and has to increase meaningfully
> I think they need to be very timely in their CPO readiness
> I think they are at the right place at the right time t

and more

I also lay out what I think is fair value today, what price I would be interested in, and what I can underwrite for price over the next 12 to 24 months

you can read it here :

https://t.co/3zoN5beZEh

text:
The FY2025 results for $IQE were published a few days ago

The headline looks weak:

- Revenue: £97.3M, down 17.6% YoY

- Adjusted EBITDA: £3.2M

What you need to know:

- Photonics Revenue: £57.1M, up 15% YoY

Driven by US defense funding and accelerated AI and data center demand for InP solutions

- Wireless: £40.1M, down 40%

The inventory correction that everyone already knew about

- Operating Cash Flow: £8.1M, up from £1.3M in FY2024

- Capex: Reduced from £11.4M to £5.1M the business is generating cash

Now, the 2026 guidance, this is the number that matters:

- Expected Revenue: To exceed 20% YoY growth

- Adjusted EBITDA: High single digits to low double digits

- Solid order book visibility into the second half of the year

CEO Jutta Meier: "Accelerated demand for our InP solutions supporting the data centre and AI markets is expected to be a material growth driver through 2026 and beyond”

Multiple Tier 1 Photonics InP design wins for next generation optical interconnects

- GaN-on-Si MicroLEDs addressing data center networking bottlenecks

- Second generation 3D sensing VCSEL in production for a leading global smartphone platform

Even though the wireless segment is dragging down the results and muddying the waters

It is an outdated branch, and $IQE is not being valued at these current levels because of it

The expectations and numbers for the branch we actually care about are doing incredibly well

On top of that, it seems the stock dilution at 20p from $MTSI has already been priced in

As a result, the debt has now been cleared thanks to this, and the dilution seems to be behind us

I see it rallying hard from here, without stopping for weeks
